name: Pasta and Broccoli
recipeIngredient: 1 large broccoli bunch, with at least 4 stems
20 large sprigs Italian parsley, leaves only, coarsely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. This is Giordano's version of the dish, which I liked the best of the six I tried.
2. Clean broccoli by removing tough bottom stems and separating stems from florets. Place in 2 bowls of cold water for 30 minutes.
3. Bring a large pot of water to boil. Add coarse salt to taste, then add pasta and immediately afterward, broccoli stems. Cook until al dente (9-12 minutes). About 2 minutes after adding the stems, add the florets.
4. Meanwhile, mince garlic.
5. Place oil in a small saucepan over medium heat. When oil is warm, add garlic and sauté until lightly golden, about 1 minute. Season with salt, pepper, and red pepper flakes. Add capers and continue to sauté for another 2 minutes.
6. Drain the pasta and broccoli from the pot of boiling water. Transfer to a large warmed serving dish.
7. Pour the sauce over the pasta and mix well. Sprinkle parsley over and serve immediately.
8. Makes 4-6 servings.
9. Each serving contains about: 825 calories; 374 milligrams sodium; 0 cholesterol; 43 grams fat; 95 grams carbohydrates; 19 grams protein; 2.1 grams fiber.
